ks1.objective: Dribble around the area and free frozen teammates with a high five.
successCriteria: Keeps the ball close while moving
Stops the ball under control when tagged
Gives clear high fives to free friends

lks2.objective: Maintain dribble control while avoiding taggers, and react positively by freeing frozen teammates quickly.
successCriteria: Foot on the ball when tagged — under control

uks2.objective: Use awareness, ball control and quick decisions to manage the dual job of dribbling and freeing teammates under pressure.
successCriteria: Performs a turn or skill before freeing

space.description: Square area defined by giant cones. Sized to your group — enough room to dribble safely without bunching.

value.name: Positivity
value.text: Spreading positivity through simple actions — a high five, a smile, an encouraging word can make a big difference. These small actions build confidence, enjoyment and a positive environment for everyone.

steps: Choose 2 children as High Five Taggers (bibs on).
The rest dribble inside the area with a football.
When tagged, the runner stops their football under control — one foot on the ball — and freezes.
To rejoin: another child dribbles over, arrives under control, and gives a high five.
Both players immediately rejoin the game.
Rotate the taggers regularly to keep the tempo high.
progressions: Start with free dribbling — focus on awareness and avoiding the taggers.
Progress so freeing requires a high TEN (two-handed) — ball must be under even greater control.
Require a trick or skill move before giving the high five.
Weaker foot only when dribbling to free a teammate.
questions: What do you do with the ball when you get tagged?
How can you stay positive when you're frozen?
What helps you spot a frozen teammate quickly?

step.space: Reduce the area to increase pressure, scanning and decision-making
step.task: Combine a turn and acceleration before freeing a teammate
step.equipment: Add gates that children must dribble through before giving a high five
step.people: Make the taggers dribble with a football too — challenge for attackers and defenders

base.showmanship: Give children lots of high fives throughout the session. The children look up to you, so role-model the behaviours you want them to show.
